entity.name: US Patent and Trademark Office
entity.description: Sets policy on AI-assisted invention (Thaler/DABUS aftermath) and AI-generated work copyrightability (jointly with Copyright Office). Issued 2024 inventorship guidance requiring significant human contribution. Decisions ripple into training-data fair-use and authorship debates.

activity.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Submission to the Office of Management and Budget for Review and Approval; Comment Request; Patent Trial and Appeal Board (PTAB) Appeals
activity.raw_text:
Agency Information Collection Activities; Submission to the Office of Management and Budget for Review and Approval; Comment Request; Patent Trial and Appeal Board (PTAB) Appeals

The United States Patent and Trademark Office (hereafter "USPTO" or "Agency"), as required by the Paperwork Reduction Act of 1995, invites comments on the extension and revision of an existing information collection: 0651-0063 (Patent Trial and Appeal Board (PTAB) Appeals). The purpose of this notice is to allow 60 days for public comments preceding submission of the information collection to the Office of Management and Budget (OMB).

Notice of information collection; request for comments.

payload.action: Notice of information collection; request for comments.

enrichment.summary: The USPTO published a request for public comments under the Paperwork Reduction Act on the extension and revision of an existing information collection (OMB control number 0651-0063) related to Patent Trial and Appeal Board (PTAB) Appeals. The notice opens a 60-day public comment period before the collection is submitted to the Office of Management and Budget for review and approval. This is a routine administrative notice with no substantive AI policy content.

activity.title: Request for Comments on Community Outreach Office Locations in the Southeast Region
activity.raw_text:
Request for Comments on Community Outreach Office Locations in the Southeast Region

The United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO or Office) is seeking information to consider regarding the selection of locations for one or more community outreach offices (COOs) in the Southeast Region, which the USPTO was directed to establish under the Unleashing American Innovators Act of 2022 (UAIA). This region includes Virginia, North Carolina, South Carolina, Georgia, Florida, Tennessee, Alabama, Mississippi, Louisiana, and Arkansas.

Request for comments.

enrichment.summary: The USPTO issued a Request for Comments seeking public input on the selection of locations for one or more community outreach offices (COOs) in the Southeast Region. This action was directed by the Unleashing American Innovators Act of 2022 (UAIA). The covered region includes Virginia, North Carolina, South Carolina, Georgia, Florida, Tennessee, Alabama, Mississippi, Louisiana, and Arkansas. As an RFI, the USPTO is gathering information and has not taken a policy position.

activity.title: Revised Inventorship Guidance for AI-Assisted Inventions
activity.raw_text:
Revised Inventorship Guidance for AI-Assisted Inventions

The United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) had issued inventorship guidance for AI-assisted inventions on February 13, 2024.\1\ The USPTO hereby rescinds the previously published Inventorship Guidance for AI-Assisted Inventions and replaces it with the guidance below. ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

Examination guidance.

enrichment.summary: The USPTO issued revised inventorship guidance for AI-assisted inventions, rescinding its prior guidance published on February 13, 2024, and replacing it with updated examination guidance. The new guidance addresses how inventorship is determined when AI systems are involved in the invention process. This constitutes a formal update to USPTO examination policy on AI-assisted inventions.
